title: rosa tomentosa f. ovoidea
intro: Rosa tomentosa f. ovoidea, also known as the Woolly Rose or Prickly Rose, is a rare, fragrant, and thorny shrub native to the Himalayas and Western China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The medicinal properties of Rosa tomentosa f. ovoidea have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering relief from pain, inflammation, and infections. Herbal preparations of Rosa tomentosa f. ovoidea include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
